original: The leyak bobs in the air in front of you, its venomous tentacles alternating between keeping it afloat and lashing through the air at you. Its translucent head ripples with unnamable hues, and its full lips are parted hungrily, twin tongues licking them in anticipation.

original: The leyak’s tentacle slaps uselessly against your airtight equipment, leaving a smear of iridescent slime. You’re thankful that it can’t seem to make it through your gear.

original: You yelp in pain as a fiery sting burns into your flesh, jarringly followed by a worrying surge of numbness. You are poisoned!

original: The tentacle slaps against your flesh, leaving a smear of quick-drying slime. Your breath catches in your throat as you feel your body flush with arousal.

original: The tentacle seems to only graze you, leaving a strange stinging that travels up your body and to your mouth. You’re uninjured, but you can’t seem to stop drooling.

original: The leyak collides into you, its lips suckered against your skin. You wince in pain as it slurps off some of your biomass. Flailing to knock it loose, only sends it swooping back up into the air. It looks ready to drop down on you again for more!

original: You lurch backward, throwing your arm up in front of your eyes a moment too late. Dazzled, you see the nauseating afterimages of the chromatic conflagration. It’ll take a few moments for your vision to return, but until then, you are blinded!

original: A myriad of colors flare across the leyak’s translucent membrane.
